news.title: China's April 2026 Export VAT Rebate Cuts Are Now in Force
news.snippet: # China Cancels Export VAT Rebates on 271 Products from April 2026. ## 249 Products Lose Rebates Overnight, 22 Battery Items Phased Out â the Export Window Is Closing Fast. **Summary:** Effective April 1, 2026, China will cancel VAT export rebates on 249 product categories spanning chemicals, solar cells, ceramics, glass, stone, and building materials. Separately, 22 battery product categories will see their rebate rate cut from 9% to 6% on the same date, then reduced to zero from January 1, 2027. These are critical raw materials for power and energy storage batteries, and the rebate cancellation will directly raise export costs across the entire lithium battery supply chain. * **Building ceramics:** ceramic tiles, floor tiles, mosaics (69072110â69074090) â China is the world's largest ceramic tile exporter; the rebate cancellation will significantly affect major production hubs such as Foshan, Chaozhou, and Zibo. * **Plan for the battery phase-out:** Battery exporters need a two-phase costing model â 6% rebate from April to December 2026, then zero from 2027.
